user_query:
I’m planning an ‘epic arcs’ Star Wars reading sprint and need a small, reproducible local dataset to organize it. Please use the seed list in input/seed_star_wars_books.csv and build a mini catalog with covers by querying only the Open Library public services (the Internet Archive’s Open Library project on the openlibrary.org domain): the Books API by ISBN for bibliographic JSON and the Covers service for images.

Requirements:
1) Read input/seed_star_wars_books.csv (columns: arc_name, canon_or_legends, title, author, isbn13).
2) For each ISBN13:
   - Download its bibliographic JSON from the Open Library Books API by ISBN and save it to data/raw/openlibrary/{isbn13}.json (one file per book).
   - Download a cover image (any single representative size provided by the Open Library Covers service) and save it to assets/covers/{isbn13}.{ext}, preserving the served image format extension.
3) Create a processed table at data/processed/reading_arcs.csv with the following columns, one row per input ISBN:
   - isbn13 (from input)
   - arc_name (from input)
   - canon_or_legends (from input)
   - input_title (from input)
   - input_author (from input)
   - fetched_title (from Open Library, if available)
   - fetched_authors (semicolon-separated, if available)
   - publish_year (a single year if available; otherwise blank)
   - number_of_pages (integer if available; otherwise blank)
   - publishers (semicolon-separated, if available)
   - cover_image_path (relative path under assets/covers if downloaded; otherwise blank)
   - source (literal string "Open Library")
   - match_confidence (simple string label: "exact_title" if a case/punctuation-insensitive match between input_title and fetched_title; otherwise "other").
4) Also produce data/processed/arcs_summary.json mapping each arc_name to an object with: {"canon_or_legends", "book_count", "total_pages_available" (sum of number_of_pages where present), "is_trilogy" (true if book_count == 3, else false)}.
5) Log any ISBNs that failed to return bibliographic JSON or a cover image to logs/missing.txt (one ISBN per line with a short reason). Even if fetches fail, include those books in reading_arcs.csv with blanks for missing fetched fields and cover_image_path.
6) Provide a runnable script under scripts/ (for example, scripts/run.sh) that, when executed from the workspace root, (a) creates/clears data/raw, data/processed, assets/covers, and logs, (b) downloads the remote resources from Open Library based on the input file, and (c) rebuilds the processed outputs deterministically from the saved JSON. Do not require any credentials, and do not hardcode pre-fetched metadata.

Please keep all outputs within the specified paths. I want this to be something I could re-run on my own machine to refresh covers/metadata later, so make sure the script drives both the downloads and the processing. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
